what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment that points to an image asset in the design-assets channel.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content
standouts: Target URI contains a space and parentheses: image://design-assets/icons/image (45)
It is a minimal metadata stub for a design-assets image attachment
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added keys
Target uses an image:// URI scheme
Markdown file acts as a ledger/metadata entry
concerns: Target URI includes unescaped spaces and parentheses, which may cause parsing or resolution issues
No title, alt text, mime type, size, or uploader fields are provided
No body or contextual description beyond the metadata fields
notes: A bare ledger stub that records an image attachment reference in the design-assets channel with a URI and timestamp.
questions: Is the target URI intended to be human-readable or machine-resolvable?
Which channel message or conversation is this attachment associated with?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is an attachment metadata record; actual content matches that purpose but is minimal and lacks descriptive asset fields.
intention_signals: It appears to track when a design-assets image was attached to a channel, giving it a stable id and a pointer to the stored image file.
novelty: The target URI embeds a literal space and parentheses around a numeric suffix, which is unusual for a clean resource locator.
contribution: Provides a dated, typed, URI-based reference for a design-assets image attachment that a fused system can use to locate, version, and correlate the asset.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter ledger record for attachment att-69809dfe in the design-assets channel, typed as images, pointing to image://design-assets/icons/image (45) and dated 2026-06-22T12:26:26.182020+00:00.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog a specific image attachment reference in the design-assets channel with a stable id, type, target URI, and creation timestamp.
